Bug Checker — System Architecture

python .github/bug_checker/run_bug_checker.py

Input Acquisition
CI Path
fetch_pr_info(pr_number)
github_client.py : 41–76
Triggered by --pr 1234
Uses gh CLI to hit GitHub API.
Gets real PR title, labels, SHAs, diff, file list.
gh pr view · gh pr diff
Local Path
fetch_branch_diff(base)
github_client.py : 79–129
Triggered by --branch
Uses local git commands.
Computes merge-base, diff, changed files.
PR# = 0, labels = [] unless --labels.
git merge-base · git diff
both produce PRInfo dataclass
Shared Pipeline
Orchestrator
run_bug_check(pr_info, sarif_path, post_comments)
orchestrator.py : 21–89
Central coordinator. Sequences: load rules → select matching → group by model → per-rule LLM analysis → collect findings → dispatch outputs.
Rule Engine
load_rules() → select_rules() → group_rules()
rules.py + manifest.yaml + rules/*.md
manifest.yaml declares each rule’s file, severity, paths, labels, model, group.
select_rules filters by file-glob + label match against PRInfo.
group_rules batches rules sharing a group into one LLM session.
  • ccl-ring-buffer-mismatch — blocking, paths: ccl/**
  • reshape-dim-check — warning, paths: data_movement/**
LLM Analysis
LLMSession.analyze_rule()
llm.py : 31–194
Creates Anthropic client (Claude). Per matched rule:
1. _filter_diff_for_rule() narrows diff to relevant files
2. Builds system prompt (structured ```finding format)
3. Builds user message: rule .md content + filtered diff
4. Calls client.messages.create() at temperature=0
5. Parses response into Finding objects
model: claude-sonnet-4-6 · max_tokens: 4096
Output Dispatch
Outputs
Three output channels
output.py + github_client.py
  • CLI stdout — print_findings() — always runs, colorized via loguru
  • SARIF file — write_sarif() — if --sarif, for GitHub Code Scanning
  • PR comments — post_pr_comment() — if --post-comments, inline + summary
Exit Code
exit 1 if any blocking finding, else exit 0
__main__.py : 86–87
CI input (GitHub API)
Local input (git CLI)
Shared pipeline
LLM (Claude API)
Output channels
